channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
item.description: Replit's CEO Amjad Masad discussed the potential acquisition of rival Cursor by SpaceX for $60 billion and his preference to keep Replit independent. This highlights the competitive landscape in the AI and coding platform market.

item.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
item.description: Meta has acquired the robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence to enhance its humanoid AI capabilities. This move signifies Meta's commitment to advancing AI in robotics.

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
item.description: During the first week of the trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk claimed he was misled into funding OpenAI and warned about the dangers of AI. This legal battle could have significant implications for AI governance.

item.title: ServiceNow under siege as Atlassian adds to ITSM take-outs
item.description: Atlassian reported its largest quarter for displacing competitors in IT service management, intensifying its competition with ServiceNow. This reflects a shifting landscape in ITSM solutions.

item.title: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex
item.description: GitHub announced the deprecation of GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex models across its Copilot services, signaling a shift in their AI offerings. Users will need to adapt to these changes.

item.title: T-Mobile is giving 5G customers Hulu and Netflix subscriptions, free - here's how to get them
item.description: T-Mobile is now offering free Hulu and Netflix subscriptions to customers on select 5G plans. This promotion aims to enhance customer satisfaction and loyalty.

item.title: A Dark-Money Campaign Is Paying Influencers to Frame Chinese AI as a Threat
item.description: A campaign funded by a nonprofit linked to OpenAI executives is promoting pro-AI messaging while framing Chinese AI as a threat. This raises concerns about the influence of money in shaping public perception of AI.

item.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
item.description: Ubuntu infrastructure has experienced a significant outage lasting over a day, affecting communication about a critical vulnerability. This incident underscores the importance of reliable infrastructure.

item.title: 5 last-minute gifts your mom will actually like for Mother's Day (and all are on sale)
item.description: An article provides suggestions for last-minute Mother's Day gifts that are on sale, focusing on practical gadgets and cozy items. This caters to consumers looking for gift ideas.

item.title: AWS Transform now automates BI migration to Amazon Quick in days
item.description: AWS Transform has introduced automation for business intelligence migration to Amazon Quick, streamlining the process for organizations. This advancement could enhance data consumption efficiency.

item.title: Samsung will give you a free 32-inch Odyssey monitor right now - how to qualify
item.description: Samsung has reintroduced its buy-one-get-one (BOGO) deal, offering customers a free 32-inch Odyssey monitor with qualifying purchases. This promotion aims to boost sales and attract more customers.

item.title: All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The trial between Elon Musk and Sam Altman is revealing key evidence, including emails and documents from OpenAI's early days. This case could impact the future of AI governance and corporate accountability.

item.title: How I scan documents with my Android phone and turn them into PDFs for free - it's easy
item.description: A guide on using Google's free tool to scan documents and convert them into PDFs on Android phones has been shared, highlighting its ease of use. This functionality can enhance productivity for mobile users.

item.title: I used Photoshop's new AI tool to rotate objects in 3D, and it's pure magic
item.description: Adobe's new AI tool in Photoshop allows users to rotate objects in 3D, showcasing impressive capabilities. However, the article emphasizes that human skill remains essential for optimal results.

item.title: I replaced my dad's aging Lenovo desktop with this Dell model - and don't regret it
item.description: A review of a Dell 24-inch All-in-One desktop highlights its compact design and reliable performance, making it a suitable replacement for an aging Lenovo model. However, it does have some limitations.

item.title: Show HN: Gemini free tier is all you need
item.description: An article discusses the Gemini free tier, suggesting it provides all necessary features for users. This could attract new users to the platform without upfront costs.

item.title: quredec-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The MCP server for QuReDec has been added to PyPI, offering structured decision briefs with citations. This tool aims to enhance decision-making processes in AI applications.

item.title: xai-eval added to PyPI
item.description: A new stress-testing framework for evaluating Explainable AI (XAI) has been added to PyPI. This tool aims to assess the divergence in explanations provided by AI systems.

item.title: These prep school students can't read this basic sentence
item.description: A viral video highlights declining reading proficiency among youth in the US, with recent assessments showing historic lows in literacy skills. This raises concerns about educational standards.

item.title: Tim Cook’s advice to incoming Apple CEO John Ternus
item.description: Tim Cook has shared advice with incoming Apple CEO John Ternus, emphasizing the importance of adhering to Apple's guiding principles. This guidance reflects the company's commitment to its core values.

item.title: S&P, Nasdaq rethink rules to speed entry of mega IPOs into key indices
item.description: The S&P and Nasdaq are changing their rules to allow mega IPOs from companies like SpaceX and OpenAI to enter key indices more quickly. This includes shorter eligibility timelines and relaxed profitability criteria, reflecting the growing importance of these firms in the market.

item.title: sage-ai-cli 1.18.2
item.description: Sage AI has released version 1.18.2 of its local-first AI coding CLI, which operates similarly to Claude Code and utilizes free/open models. This update enhances the tool's capabilities for developers.

item.title: White House blocks Anthropic’s Mythos expansion over security concerns
item.description: The White House has blocked Anthropic's expansion of its Mythos AI system due to national security concerns. This decision highlights the U.S. government's focus on regulating AI amid global competition.

item.title: Are Elon Musk and OpenAI Fighting an AI Arms Race? Sam Altman’s Lawyers Think So
item.description: During the OpenAI trial, Elon Musk faced tough questioning from lawyers representing Sam Altman and Microsoft, suggesting a fierce competition in the AI sector. The trial reflects the ongoing tensions in the AI arms race.

item.title: claude-fieldnotes 0.7.1
item.description: Claude has released version 0.7.1 of its fieldnotes, which are repo-scoped notes about a codebase, designed to be SHA-pinned and drift-aware. This update aims to enhance code documentation.

item.title: Re: IPv8 / BGP8 / CF
item.description: A discussion on IPv8 and BGP8 was posted by Andrew Kirch, emphasizing a point made earlier. The thread is suggested to be concluded, indicating a consensus on the topic.

item.title: sage-ai-cli 1.18.1
item.description: Sage AI has also released version 1.18.1 of its local-first AI coding CLI, similar to Claude Code and utilizing free/open models. This version continues to support developers in coding tasks.

item.title: langchain-velesdb 1.14.1
item.description: LangChain has released version 1.14.1 of its VectorStore for VelesDB, a local AI memory database that offers microsecond retrieval for RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) tasks. This improves data handling for AI applications.

item.title: llama-index-vector-stores-velesdb 1.14.1
item.description: LlamaIndex has released version 1.14.1 of its VectorStore for VelesDB, also focusing on microsecond retrieval for RAG tasks. This update supports local AI memory management.

item.title: Netomi banks $110M to embed agentic AI deeper into enterprise customer service
item.description: Netomi has secured $110 million in funding to enhance its agentic AI platform for enterprise customer service. This investment aims to accelerate deployment in high-volume, regulated environments.

item.title: Apple earnings beat forecasts on iPhone 17 demand
item.description: Apple reported a record profit of $29.6 billion on $111.2 billion in revenue, driven by strong iPhone 17 demand and digital services. This performance marks the company's best start to the year, exceeding expectations.

item.title: Apple iPhone powers quarterly sales to $111B: ‘Demand was off the charts’
item.description: iPhone sales reached $56.99 billion, contributing significantly to Apple's quarterly revenue of $111 billion. The demand for the iPhone remains exceptionally high nearly two decades after its launch.

item.title: Agentic opportunity: OpenAI and Stripe build for rising tide of new entrepreneurial firms
item.description: OpenAI and Stripe are collaborating to support the growth of new entrepreneurial firms, showcasing a significant upward trend in startup metrics. This partnership aims to leverage AI and financial technology for innovation.

item.title: Gemini can now generate files for you
item.description: Google's Gemini now offers the ability to generate various file types, including documents and spreadsheets, enhancing its utility for users. This feature is available globally, expanding Gemini's functionality.

item.title: hol-guard 2.0.87
item.description: HOL Guard has been updated to protect local AI harnesses before executing tools like Codex and Gemini. This update aims to enhance security for AI applications.

item.title: jang 2.5.9
item.description: JANG version 2.5.9 introduces Adaptive Mixed-Precision Quantization for Apple Silicon, enhancing machine learning capabilities. This update supports various models, improving performance.

item.title: hol-guard 2.0.86
item.description: HOL Guard has received another update aimed at securing local AI harnesses before running various tools. This version focuses on maintaining the integrity of AI operations.

item.title: langchain-tool-near-state-reader added to PyPI
item.description: The LangChain Tool - NEAR State Reader has been added to PyPI, expanding the toolkit available for developers working with NEAR protocol. This addition enhances the ecosystem for blockchain applications.

item.title: langchain-tool-near-state-reader 1.0.0
item.description: The LangChain Tool - NEAR State Reader has been released in version 1.0.0, providing developers with new capabilities for interacting with NEAR protocol. This tool aims to streamline development processes.

item.title: Apple hits record earnings as iPhone 17 demand surges
item.description: Apple's record earnings for the March quarter are attributed to soaring iPhone 17 demand, highlighting the company's financial strength. However, it also faces future challenges in maintaining this momentum.

item.title: iOS 27 Could Bring Siri Apple Intelligence Support To The iPhone’s Camera App
item.description: iOS 27 may integrate Siri with Apple Intelligence into the iPhone Camera app, enhancing user interaction and functionality. This could significantly improve how users capture and manage photos.

item.title: langchain-fireworks 1.2.1
item.description: The langchain-fireworks 1.2.1 package connects Fireworks and LangChain, facilitating better integration for developers. This could streamline workflows in AI applications.

item.title: Elon Musk spars with OpenAI attorney in trial over company's evolution from a nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk clashed with an OpenAI attorney during his testimony in a trial about OpenAI's transition from nonprofit to for-profit, highlighting tensions over the company's evolution. This trial could impact perceptions of AI governance.

item.title: langchain-arcgate added to PyPI
item.description: The langchain-arcgate package has been added to PyPI, providing prompt injection detection for LangChain. This tool aims to enhance security in AI applications.

item.title: Why AI systems fail and how to build ones that don’t
item.description: The article discusses common reasons why AI systems fail and offers strategies to build more reliable systems. Understanding these failures is crucial for developers aiming to improve AI performance.

item.title: numbast 0.8.0
item.description: Numbast 0.8.0 is an auto Numba binding generation tool for CUDA C++, aimed at simplifying the development process for programmers. This tool could enhance productivity in AI and machine learning projects.

item.title: Mistral AI Drops New Open-Source Model. The Internet Is Not Impressed, Except for One Thing
item.description: Mistral AI has released a new open-source model, Medium 3.5, which, despite being a notable Western entry, is criticized for its high cost compared to better-performing Chinese models. This raises questions about competitiveness in the AI market.

item.title: XMax Inc. Launches AI Inference Platform and New AI and Corporate Websites
item.description: XMax Inc. launched its AI Inference Platform and new corporate websites, aiming to enhance its AI capabilities and market presence. This move could position XMax as a player in the AI industry.

item.title: This is Tim (and John and Kevin): Transcript of Apple’s Q2 2026 financial call
item.description: In Apple's Q2 2026 financial call, CEO Tim Cook and CFO Kevan Parekh discussed the company's performance and future outlook, revealing insights into Apple's ongoing strategies and successes. This information is valuable for investors and tech enthusiasts.

item.title: "I just wanted to take Solstice ... and kind of blow it up a little bit": This solo Xbox dev has built a puzzle gauntlet with metroidvania leanings, and it's coming soon
item.description: A solo developer has created a puzzle game called OOLO, inspired by the NES game Solstice, featuring voxel puzzles and metroidvania elements. This indie project showcases creativity in game development.
